assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 marked 'YES' (h2 0.4.5, below fix threshold 0.4.8) and all 2.2.x versions (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) marked 'NO' (h2 >= 0.4.8). Summary states 'Mixed impact across streams.' Lock file analysis is cited as the basis (h2 versions from Cargo.lock at pinned commits).

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows proposed correction: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.2.0] -> Proposed: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]'. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly removed ('ships h2 0.4.8 (at fix threshold)') and RHTPA 2.1.1 is added ('ships h2 0.4.5 (below fix threshold 0.4.8)'). Only 2.1.x versions remain in the corrected set.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates exactly two tasks for 2.1.x: Task 1 (Upstream Backport targeting release/0.3.z) and Task 2 (Downstream Propagation for rhtpa-release.0.3.z). A dedicated section titled 'No Remediation for 2.2.x Stream' explicitly states 'No tasks are created for this stream' with a table showing all 2.2.x versions ship h2 >= 0.4.8.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'Product version (PSIRT-claimed): (no stream suffix -- unscoped)' and 'this issue is unscoped -- it covers all configured version streams.' Both streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x) are listed under 'Configured streams' and the note says 'Both streams will be analyzed in Step 2.' The version impact table in version-impact.md confirms all 7 versions across both streams were checked.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md explicitly states: 'Since the issue is unscoped (covers all streams), there is no cross-stream impact notice (Case B does not apply). The unscoped issue already spans all streams, so there are no "other streams outside this issue's scope."' No cross-stream impact notice or sibling issue creation appears anywhere in the outputs.
